dimension: @sodax/types — the typed API contract (Swaps API v2 pattern, to be mirrored for Bridge API v2)

files_read.role: The full Swaps API v2 + Config API v2 wire contract (branch origin/feat/swaps-api-v2). Holds every *RequestV2/*ResponseV2 type, the ISwapsApiV2 and IConfigApiV2 aggregating interfaces, and the compile-time JSON-safety drift guard. THIS is the file the Bridge contract must mirror.

Config-only v1 contract on the branch (renamed/trimmed from backendApi.ts). Exposes IConfigApiV1 + the legacy GetAllConfigApiResponse with supportedChains/supportedSwapTokens/... fields. The swap submit-tx types that were in HEAD:backendApi.ts were DROPPED here (superseded by V2).
HEAD (working-tree) version — a mixed bag: IConfigApi + SwapIntentData + SubmitSwapTx* legacy types. The branch renames it to backendApiV1.ts and trims it to config-only.
Barrel. Branch changes `export * from './backendApi.js'` -> `'./backendApiV1.js'` and keeps `'./backendApiV2.js'`.

Defines RawTxReturnType union, HashTxReturnType, GetDefaultTxReturnType<Raw>, TxReturnType<C,Raw>, PartnerFee, MoneyMarket*Config, Result, SolverIntentStatus/ErrorCode. RawTxReturnType is the type used for the unsigned `tx` on responses.
Config types: split ApiConfig into BaseApiConfig / SwapsApiConfig / CustomApiConfig / ApiConfig (discriminated union). Holds DEFAULT_BACKEND_API_HEADERS, apiConfig const, RelayConfig, SolverConfig.

Top-level config tree: SodaxDefaultConfig, SodaxOptions, SodaxOptionalConfig, SodaxConfig. Branch adds SwapsClientOptions + `swapsOptions?` slot. Holds BridgeDefaultConfig={}, BridgeOptions={partnerFee?}, BridgeConfig.
SodaxFeature string union. Branch adds `'backend'` member (the HTTP client layer).
RelayChainIdMap, IntentRelayChainId, ChainKey. Branch only fixes IntentRelayChainIdToChainKey to use `new Map(...)` instead of Object.fromEntries (typing fix, not feature).
SolanaRawTransaction = {from; to; value:bigint; data:Base64String}. Branch adds `signAndSendTransaction?(params: SolanaRawTransaction): Promise<TransactionSignature>` to ISolanaWalletProvider.
StacksRawTransaction = {payload:string; estimatedLength?:number}. Branch adds `signAndSendTransaction?(params: StacksRawTransaction): Promise<string>` to IStacksWalletProvider.

StellarRawTransaction = {from; to; value:bigint; data:string}. Branch adds `sendTransaction?(signedTx: XDR): Promise<string>` and `signAndSendTransaction?(params: StellarRawTransaction): Promise<string>` to IStellarWalletProvider.

key_findings: ISwapsApiV2 (backendApiV2.ts lines 684-727) is an interface with ONE async method per backend endpoint, all returning Promise<...ResponseV2>. Full method list with exact signatures: getTokens(): Promise<GetSwapTokensResponseV2>; getTokensByChain(chainKey: string): Promise<GetSwapTokensByChainResponseV2>; getQuote(body: QuoteRequestV2, query?: QuoteQueryV2): Promise<QuoteResponseV2>; getDeadline(query?: DeadlineQueryV2): Promise<DeadlineResponseV2>; checkAllowance(body: CreateIntentParamsV2): Promise<AllowanceCheckResponseV2>; approve(body: CreateIntentParamsV2): Promise<ApproveResponseV2>; createIntent(body: CreateIntentParamsV2): Promise<CreateIntentResponseV2>; submitIntent(body: SubmitIntentRequestV2): Promise<SubmitIntentResponseV2>; getStatus(body: StatusRequestV2): Promise<StatusResponseV2>; cancelIntent(body: CancelIntentRequestV2): Promise<CancelIntentResponseV2>; getIntentHash(body: IntentHashRequestV2): Promise<IntentHashResponseV2>; getSolvedIntentPacket(body: IntentPacketRequestV2): Promise<IntentPacketResponseV2>; getIntentSubmitTxExtraData(body: IntentExtraDataRequestV2): Promise<IntentExtraDataResponseV2>; getFilledIntent(txHash: string): Promise<IntentStateV2>; getIntent(txHash: string): Promise<GetIntentResponseV2>; createLimitOrderIntent(body: CreateLimitOrderParamsV2): Promise<CreateLimitOrderResponseV2>; estimateGas(body: GasEstimateRequestV2): Promise<GasEstimateResponseV2>; getPartnerFee(query: FeeQueryV2): Promise<FeeResponseV2>; getSolverFee(query: FeeQueryV2): Promise<FeeResponseV2>; submitTx(body: SubmitTxRequestV2): Promise<SubmitTxResponseV2>; getSubmitTxStatus(query: SubmitTxStatusQueryV2): Promise<SubmitTxStatusResponseV2>.

Naming/structure pattern is rigid: every endpoint gets a `*RequestV2` (POST body) and/or `*QueryV2` (query params) and a `*ResponseV2` interface, each preceded by a `/** POST /swaps/... — request body. */` doc comment, and grouped under a banner comment block (`// ──── POST /swaps/quote ────`). Shapes used in BOTH directions are split into separate Request/Response interfaces (e.g. IntentRequestV2 vs IntentResponseV2) even when only numeric typing differs.

THE CENTRAL bigint/JSON rule (file header lines 8-27): Response (outbound) types are pure JSON — every bigint-derived value (intent ids, amounts, deadlines, fees, relay chain ids) is a decimal `string` and every Date is an ISO-8601 `string`; a response NEVER contains bigint on the wire. Request (inbound) types mirror the server's parsed DTOs: IntentRequestV2 carries `bigint` numeric fields (matches backend IntentDto), while other request fields (amounts/deadlines on CreateIntentParamsV2/QuoteRequestV2) are decimal `string` (matches @IsNumberString DTO fields). Hex/Address/Hash/SpokeChainKey are plain `string` everywhere.

THE UNSIGNED TX TYPE (the key change from the diff): every endpoint that returns an unsigned transaction types it as `tx: RawTxReturnType` (imported `import type { RawTxReturnType } from '../common/index.js';` at line 45). Before this PR these were `tx: unknown`. Affected: ApproveResponseV2.tx, CreateIntentResponseV2.tx, CancelIntentResponseV2.tx. Doc comment convention: `/** Unsigned ...-intent transaction — the RawTxReturnType variant for the request's srcChainKey. */`. The tx travels as pure JSON (bigints stringified, Injective byte arrays as {"0":N,...} index objects) and the SDK client's response schema (@sodax/sdk rawTxSchemas) rebuilds bigint/Uint8Array on parse — inverse of the backend's stringifyBigInts. Other opaque payloads (gas estimate `gas`, relay `result`) stay `unknown`.

RawTxReturnType (common/common.ts lines 186-194) = EvmRawTransaction | SolanaRawTransaction | InjectiveRawTransaction | IconRawTransaction | SuiRawTransaction | StellarRawTransaction | StacksRawTransaction | NearRawTransaction (8 members; NOTE: Bitcoin is NOT in this union). Each per-chain RawTransaction already existed pre-PR. Shapes: EvmRawTransaction={from:Address;to:Address;value:bigint;data:Hex}; SolanaRawTransaction={from;to;value:bigint;data:Base64String}; StellarRawTransaction={from;to;value:bigint;data:string}; StacksRawTransaction={payload:string;estimatedLength?:number}. RawTxReturnType is reusable as-is for Bridge — no new per-chain raw-tx types need to be authored.

Per-chain WALLET PROVIDER additions (the only per-chain type changes the PR made): added optional `signAndSendTransaction?` to wallet provider interfaces so the frontend can deserialize+sign+broadcast the unsigned tx returned by the API. ISolanaWalletProvider: `signAndSendTransaction?: (params: SolanaRawTransaction) => Promise<TransactionSignature>`. IStacksWalletProvider: `signAndSendTransaction?: (params: StacksRawTransaction) => Promise<string>`. IStellarWalletProvider: `sendTransaction?: (signedTx: XDR) => Promise<string>` AND `signAndSendTransaction?: (params: StellarRawTransaction) => Promise<string>`. (EVM/others rely on existing send paths.) These are optional methods so they are non-breaking.

Shared building blocks in backendApiV2.ts (lines 51-219, reusable by Bridge): QuoteTypeV2='exact_input'; PartnerFeeV2 = {address:string;amount:string} | {address:string;percentage:number} (JSON mirror of SDK PartnerFee, bigint amount->string); BitcoinBoundExtrasV2 {accessToken?}; SwapExtrasV2 {partnerFee?:PartnerFeeV2; srcPublicKey?:string; bound?:BitcoinBoundExtrasV2}; SwapIntentStatusCodeV2 = -1|1|2|3|4; SwapTokenV2 (XToken projected to primitives: symbol,name,decimals,address,chainKey,hubAsset,vault all string/number); IntentRequestV2 (bigint numerics) vs IntentResponseV2 (all string); RelayExtraDataRequestV2/ResponseV2 (identical {address,payload} pair, kept separate to mirror backend DTO pairs).

submit-tx FALLBACK family (lines 558-666) — central to backend/frontend interplay: SubmitTxRequestV2 {txHash:string; srcChainKey:string; walletAddress:string; intent:IntentRequestV2; relayData:string}; SubmitTxResponseDataV2 {status:'inserted'|'duplicate'; message:string}; SubmitTxResponseV2 {success:boolean; data:SubmitTxResponseDataV2}; SubmitTxStatusQueryV2 {txHash; srcChainKey}; SubmitSwapTxStatusV2 = 'pending'|'relaying'|'relayed'|'posting_execution'|'executed'|'failed'; PacketDataStatusV2='pending'|'validating'|'executing'|'executed'; PacketDataV2 (snake_case as stored: src_chain_id, src_tx_hash, ...); SubmitTxStatusResultV2 {dstIntentTxHash; packetData?; intent_hash?}; SubmitTxStatusDataV2 {txHash; srcChainKey; status; failedAtStep?; failureReason?; processingAttempts:number; abandonedAt?:string(ISO); result?; userMessage?; intentCancelled?}; SubmitTxStatusResponseV2 {success:boolean; data:SubmitTxStatusDataV2}. This pair (submitTx + getSubmitTxStatus) is what useBackendSubmitTx polls.

V1/V2 SPLIT RATIONALE: HEAD's `backendApi.ts` mixed config (IConfigApi + GetAllConfigApiResponse) and ad-hoc swap submit-tx types (SwapIntentData, SubmitSwapTxRequest/Response, SubmitSwapTxStatus*). The PR (1) renames backendApi.ts -> backendApiV1.ts and TRIMS it to config-only (IConfigApiV1 + Get*ApiResponse + the legacy GetAllConfigApiResponse with supportedChains/supportedSwapTokens/supportedMoneyMarketTokens/supportedMoneyMarketReserveAssets/relayChainIdMap/spokeChainConfig fields), DROPPING the loose swap submit-tx types; (2) supersedes everything with backendApiV2.ts which re-declares the full swaps surface with strict request/response separation + a Config API v2 section; (3) index.ts switches the barrel to backendApiV1.js + backendApiV2.js. So V1 = legacy config contract kept for back-compat; V2 = canonical, fully-typed, one-type-per-endpoint contract.

backendApiV2.ts also contains a SECOND aggregating interface IConfigApiV2 (lines 873-892) for GET /config/* endpoints, plus its response types (GetAllConfigResponseV2, GetChainsResponseV2, GetSpokeChainConfigsResponseV2, GetSwapTokensConfigResponseV2, GetMoneyMarketTokens*, GetMoneyMarketReserveAssetsResponseV2, GetRelayChainIdMapResponseV2). The config section uses a DIFFERENT strategy than swaps: it REUSES canonical config types (SodaxDefaultConfig, SpokeChainConfig, XToken) and projects only bigint fields to string via Omit&{...} (RelayConfigV2, ConcentratedLiquidityConfigV2, DexConfigV2, SodaxConfigV2). The two bigint spots projected: relay.relayChainIdMap (Record<...,bigint>->Record<string,string>) and dex.concentratedLiquidityConfig.defaultBitmap (bigint->string).

COMPILE-TIME DRIFT GUARD (lines 894-930): non-exported helpers `_ContainsBigint<T>` (recursive type that resolves true if a bigint appears anywhere) and `_AssertJsonSafe<_Ok extends true> = unknown`. Wired into exported types as an erasable `& _AssertJsonSafe<[_ContainsBigint<X>] extends [false] ? true : false>` intersection on GetAllConfigResponseV2 (guards SodaxConfigV2) and on CreateLimitOrderParamsV2 (guards SwapExtrasV2). If a bigint ever leaks into a JSON-safe wire type, `pnpm checkTs`/`pnpm build` go red. Only `type` aliases (not interfaces) can carry the intersection — that's why CreateLimitOrderParamsV2 is a `type`.

CONFIG TYPE ADDITIONS (common/constants.ts diff): ApiConfig was a flat `{baseURL:HttpUrl; timeout:number; headers:Record<string,string>}`. Now: BaseApiConfig = that flat shape; SwapsApiConfig = BaseApiConfig (alias); CustomApiConfig = `{baseApiConfig:BaseApiConfig; swapsApiConfig?:SwapsApiConfig} | {baseApiConfig?:BaseApiConfig; swapsApiConfig:SwapsApiConfig}` (at least one slice required, discriminable by presence of baseApiConfig/swapsApiConfig since BaseApiConfig has baseURL); ApiConfig = BaseApiConfig | CustomApiConfig. This lets the SDK point base-backend API and swaps API at independent endpoints. A Bridge API endpoint would extend CustomApiConfig with a `bridgeApiConfig?` slice.

CONFIG TYPE ADDITIONS (sodax-config.ts diff): new `SwapsClientOptions = { useBackendSubmitTx?: boolean }` — client-side runtime toggle, resolved once at construction, NOT part of backend-fetched SodaxDefaultConfig. Added to SodaxOptionalConfig as `swapsOptions?: SwapsClientOptions` (DISTINCT key from the data `swaps?: SwapsOptions` slot so they never collide). Doc: 'Opt-in: route swap() through the backend submit-tx 2-step flow; on ANY non-success the SDK falls back to the client-side relay so the swap still completes. Default false.' This is the type-level anchor for the entire useBackendSubmitTx + fallback mechanism.

EXISTING BRIDGE CONFIG (sodax-config.ts, unchanged by PR): BridgeConfig = Prettify<BridgeDefaultConfig & BridgeOptions>; BridgeOptions = { partnerFee?: PartnerFee }; BridgeDefaultConfig = {} ('kept for future extension'); const bridgeConfig = {} satisfies BridgeDefaultConfig. SodaxDefaultConfig already has a `bridge: BridgeDefaultConfig` slot and SodaxOptionalConfig already has `bridge?: BridgeOptions`. So the Bridge feature already has config plumbing; what's missing is a backend wire contract (no bridge types exist in packages/types/src/backend/ today).

FEATURE FLAG (shared/features.ts diff): SodaxFeature union gained `'backend'` (comment: 'backend-API HTTP client layer (BackendApiService / SwapsApiService), not a domain feature'). The existing union already includes `'bridge'` and `'swap'`/`'swaps'`-area members. No new feature member was added for swaps-api specifically — the HTTP layer is just 'backend'.

There is NO bridge-specific file under packages/types/src/backend/ on the branch — confirmed via git ls-tree (only backendApiV1.ts, backendApiV2.ts, index.ts). All swaps contract types live inside the single backendApiV2.ts (931 lines / ~47.9 KB). The swaps controller is referenced as backend `apps/swaps-api/src/api/swaps/swaps.controller.ts` in the file header; the Bridge equivalent would be a bridge controller.

IConfigApiV1 (backendApiV1.ts) signature for reference: getChains(): Promise<Result<GetChainsApiResponse>>; getSwapTokens(): Promise<Result<...>>; getSwapTokensByChainId(chainId: SpokeChainKey): Promise<Result<...>>; getMoneyMarketTokens(); getMoneyMarketTokensByChainId(chainId). Note V1 wraps responses in Result<T> whereas ISwapsApiV2 methods return the bare ResponseV2 directly (no Result wrapper) — a deliberate V2 simplification.
open_questions: The exact set of Bridge backend endpoints (and thus IBridgeApiV2 method list) is unknown from the types package alone — it must be derived from the backend bridge controller (analogous to apps/swaps-api/src/api/swaps/swaps.controller.ts). The synthesis agent should confirm the bridge controller route list before declaring IBridgeApiV2.

Whether the Bridge contract belongs in a NEW file (packages/types/src/backend/backendBridgeApiV2.ts) or co-located in backendApiV2.ts: the swaps precedent put everything in ONE file, but backendApiV2.ts is already 931 lines. Recommendation below favors a new file for separation, but this is a judgment call the synthesis plan should make explicit.

Does Bridge need a client-side toggle analogous to SwapsClientOptions.useBackendSubmitTx (e.g. a BridgeClientOptions { useBackendSubmitTx?: boolean } + `bridgeOptions?` slot on SodaxOptionalConfig)? Depends on whether the bridge flow has the same submit-tx + fallback mechanism. If yes, mirror the swapsOptions pattern exactly with a distinct key.
Does the Bridge API need its own endpoint slice in CustomApiConfig (e.g. `bridgeApiConfig?: BaseApiConfig`)? Only if bridge is served from a different base URL than the swaps/backend API.

Bitcoin is absent from the RawTxReturnType union — if Bridge supports Bitcoin sources, confirm whether BitcoinRawTransaction must be added to RawTxReturnType (a cross-cutting change touching common/common.ts), or whether bridge excludes Bitcoin.
replication_recipe: Decide file location: create a NEW file packages/types/src/backend/backendBridgeApiV2.ts mirroring backendApiV2.ts's structure (header comment block explaining the bigint/JSON-safety rule, then sections, then the IBridgeApiV2 interface). Rationale: keeps the already-931-line backendApiV2.ts focused on swaps+config and matches the V1/V2 per-concern separation philosophy. Alternative (only if the team prefers single-file): append a `// ════ Bridge API v2 ════` section inside backendApiV2.ts.

Add `import type { RawTxReturnType } from '../common/index.js';` and reuse it verbatim for every unsigned-tx response field: `tx: RawTxReturnType` with doc `/** Unsigned bridge transaction — the RawTxReturnType variant for the request's srcChainKey. */`. Do NOT invent bridge-specific raw-tx types; RawTxReturnType already covers all chain families.

For each bridge endpoint define a `BridgeXxxRequestV2` (POST body) and/or `BridgeXxxQueryV2` (query params) interface and a `BridgeXxxResponseV2` interface, each with a leading `/** POST /bridge/... — request body. */` doc comment, grouped under a banner comment (`// ──── POST /bridge/... ────`). Follow the exact bigint rule: response numeric fields are decimal `string`; request DTO numeric fields that mirror a backend bigint DTO field carry `bigint`, request fields that mirror @IsNumberString carry decimal `string`.

If bridge has a hub-side intent/transfer struct used in BOTH directions, split it into BridgeIntentRequestV2 (bigint numerics) and BridgeIntentResponseV2 (all decimal-string numerics), exactly like IntentRequestV2 / IntentResponseV2. If a shape is direction-agnostic (no bigint), still declare Request+Response aliases to mirror the backend DTO pairs (like RelayExtraDataRequestV2/ResponseV2).

Reuse the shared building blocks from backendApiV2.ts where applicable: PartnerFeeV2, SwapExtrasV2 (or author a BridgeExtrasV2 if the bridge has distinct extras), SwapTokenV2-style token projection. Import them from '../backend/backendApiV2.js' rather than re-declaring, OR if Bridge needs its own, declare BridgeTokenV2 with the same primitive-projection style.

Declare the aggregating interface `export interface IBridgeApiV2 { /* GET /bridge/... */ method(...): Promise<BridgeXxxResponseV2>; ... }` — one async method per endpoint, returning the bare *ResponseV2 (NO Result<T> wrapper, matching ISwapsApiV2's V2 simplification, not IConfigApiV1's Result wrap). Add the same 'Do NOT implements this on the NestJS controller' doc block.

If the bridge flow has a backend submit-tx + status-polling + client fallback (the useBackendSubmitTx mechanism), replicate the submit-tx family verbatim: BridgeSubmitTxRequestV2 {txHash; srcChainKey; walletAddress; intent:BridgeIntentRequestV2; relayData:string}, BridgeSubmitTxResponseV2 {success; data:{status:'inserted'|'duplicate'; message}}, BridgeSubmitTxStatusQueryV2 {txHash; srcChainKey}, a SubmitBridgeTxStatusV2 lifecycle union, PacketDataV2-style snake_case packet type, and a BridgeSubmitTxStatusResponseV2 {success; data}.
Export the new file from packages/types/src/backend/index.ts: add `export * from './backendBridgeApiV2.js';` (alongside the existing backendApiV1.js + backendApiV2.js exports).

Config plumbing: if bridge needs a client-side toggle, add `export type BridgeClientOptions = { useBackendSubmitTx?: boolean };` to sodax-config.ts and a `bridgeOptions?: BridgeClientOptions` slot on SodaxOptionalConfig (distinct key from the data `bridge?: BridgeOptions` slot — mirror the swaps/swapsOptions split exactly, with the same 'distinct key so it never collides' comment). BridgeDefaultConfig={} and bridge?:BridgeOptions already exist; do not duplicate them.

If bridge is served from its own endpoint, extend CustomApiConfig in common/constants.ts with a `bridgeApiConfig?: BaseApiConfig` member (and a `bridgeApiConfig` alias type like SwapsApiConfig). Otherwise leave ApiConfig untouched and route bridge through baseApiConfig.

If bridge supports Solana/Stacks/Stellar sources and the frontend must sign the returned RawTxReturnType, confirm the wallet-provider `signAndSendTransaction?` methods added by this PR (ISolanaWalletProvider/IStacksWalletProvider/IStellarWalletProvider) already cover the chains bridge needs — they are generic over the raw-tx type, so no bridge-specific wallet additions are needed.

If any new JSON-safe wire type is a `type` alias (not interface) and you want drift protection, intersect `& _AssertJsonSafe<[_ContainsBigint<T>] extends [false] ? true : false>` like CreateLimitOrderParamsV2 does — but the _ContainsBigint/_AssertJsonSafe helpers are non-exported and live in backendApiV2.ts, so either keep guarded bridge types in backendApiV2.ts or re-declare the helpers in backendBridgeApiV2.ts (keeping them non-exported to avoid knip 'unused export').
